oneOf.description: Shorthand: list of values with soft (default) enforcement

enforcement.description: soft (preferred, best-effort) or hard (required, may leave pods unschedulable)

tag.description: Image tag. Defaults to the chart's appVersion. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#image-names

pullPolicy.description: When to pull the image. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#image-pull-policy

command.description: Entrypoint array (overrides the container image's ENTRYPOINT). Not executed in a shell.

args.description: Arguments to the entrypoint (overrides the container image's CMD)

env.description: Environment variables map. Use value for plain strings, valueFrom for field/resource/configMap/secret key refs, or configMapRef/secretRef for bulk injection. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/

valueFrom.description: Source for the value: fieldRef, resourceFieldRef, configMapKeyRef, or secretKeyRef

configMapRef.description: Inject all keys from a ConfigMap as env vars (envFrom)

secretRef.description: Inject all keys from a Secret as env vars (envFrom)

ports.description: Map of port name → port config. The key becomes the port name in the container spec.

port.description: Port number to expose on the container

mounts.description: Volume mounts. Each entry maps a path to a source (configMap, secret, persistence key, or raw volume name).

configMap.description: ConfigMap key from config.configMaps to mount

secret.description: Secret key from config.secrets to mount

persistence.description: Persistence key from persistence map to mount

external.description: If true, references an externally-managed resource (not prefixed with release name)

subPath.description: Sub-path within the volume to mount

items.description: Key-to-path mappings for selective file projection

resources.description: CPU/memory resource requests and limits. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

resizePolicy.description: In-place resource resize policy per resource (K8s 1.35+). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/autoscaling/#in-place-resizing

restartPolicy.description: Whether the container must restart when this resource is resized

runAsUser.description: UID to run the container process as

runAsGroup.description: GID to run the container process as

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root user

allowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Allow the process to gain more privileges than its parent

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the root filesystem as read-only

capabilities.description: Linux capabilities to add or drop

localhostProfile.description: Path to a localhost seccomp profile (when type is Localhost)

liveness.description: Liveness probe — restarts the container if it fails

successThreshold.description: Consecutive successes to be considered healthy

failureThreshold.description: Consecutive failures to be considered unhealthy

readiness.description: Readiness probe — removes the pod from endpoints if it fails

successThreshold.description: Consecutive successes to be considered healthy

failureThreshold.description: Consecutive failures to be considered unhealthy

startup.description: Startup probe — delays liveness/readiness checks until it succeeds

successThreshold.description: Consecutive successes to be considered healthy

failureThreshold.description: Consecutive failures to be considered unhealthy

postStart.description: Runs immediately after a container is created

preStop.description: Runs before a container is terminated

workingDir.description: Working directory inside the container. Defaults to the image's WORKDIR.

restartPolicy.description: Set to 'Always' for native sidecar containers (K8s 1.33+). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/sidecar-containers/

x-wizard.label: Workload Type
x-wizard.description: Choose the type of Kubernetes workload you want to deploy.

x-wizard.label: Global Settings
x-wizard.description: Global labels and annotations applied to ALL generated resources.

global.description: Global labels and annotations applied to all resources generated by this chart

nodeLabels.description: Node label key mapping. Customize the Kubernetes node label keys used by nodeTargeting.

topologyRegion.description: Node label key for region topology

topologyZone.description: Node label key for zone topology

topologyRack.description: Node label key for rack topology

nodePool.description: Node label key for node pool identification

os.description: Node label key for operating system

arch.description: Node label key for CPU architecture

nodeType.description: Node label key for instance/node type

x-wizard.label: Infrastructure Settings
x-wizard.description: Customize node label keys for your cluster (used by nodeTargeting).

infraSettings.description: Infrastructure-specific settings for your cluster. Customize node label keys used by nodeTargeting.

x-wizard.label: Argo CD
x-wizard.description: Configure Argo CD integration: sync waves and sync options.

enabled.description: Enable automatic sync wave annotations on all resources

syncWaves.description: Configure automatic sync wave annotation assignment to resources

syncOptions.description: Sync options applied to all resources (e.g. ServerSideApply=true)

argocd.description: Argo CD integration settings. See https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/

containers.description: Map of container name to container spec

x-wizard.label: Containers
x-wizard.description: Define the main application containers. At least one container with an image is required.

initContainers.description: Map of init container name to container spec (processed in alphabetical key order)

x-wizard.label: Init Containers
x-wizard.description: Init containers run before the main containers start. Keys are sorted alphabetically - use numeric prefixes (01-, 02-) to control order.

x-wizard.label: Node Targeting
x-wizard.description: Control which nodes your pods can be scheduled on. Use "soft" for best-effort, "hard" for strict requirements.

restrictions.description: Pod placement restrictions using affinity/anti-affinity. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#inter-pod-affinity-and-anti-affinity

type.description: differentNodes spreads pods across nodes (anti-affinity); sameNode co-locates pods (affinity); none disables

enforcement.description: soft = preferredDuringScheduling (best-effort); hard = requiredDuringScheduling (strict, may leave pods pending)

os.description: Target operating system(s) (e.g. linux, windows). Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.os

arch.description: Target CPU architecture(s) (e.g. amd64, arm64). Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.arch

regions.description: Target cloud region(s). Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.topologyRegion

zones.description: Target availability zone(s). Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.topologyZone

racks.description: Target rack(s) for rack-aware scheduling. Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.topologyRack

nodeTypes.description: Target node/instance type(s) (e.g. m5.xlarge). Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.nodeType

nodePools.description: Target node pool(s) (e.g. default, gpu-pool). Maps to node label from infraSettings.nodeLabels.nodePool

x-wizard.label: Pod Settings
x-wizard.description: Pod-level settings that apply to all containers: security context, scheduling, tolerations, volumes.

imagePullSecrets.description: List of image pull secrets for private registries. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing registry credentials

securityContext.description: Pod-level security context (fsGroup, runAsUser, etc.). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/workload-resources/pod-v1/#security-context

runAsUser.description: UID to run all pod containers as

runAsGroup.description: GID to run all pod containers as

runAsNonRoot.description: Require all containers to run as non-root

fsGroup.description: GID applied to all volumes mounted by the pod

fsGroupChangePolicy.description: When to change ownership of mounted volumes

supplementalGroups.description: Additional GIDs applied to the first process in each container

localhostProfile.description: Path to a localhost seccomp profile (when type is Localhost)

annotations.description: Extra annotations added to the pod template

labels.description: Extra labels added to the pod template

nodeSelector.description: Simple label-based node selection. For advanced rules, use nodeTargeting instead. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodeselector

affinity.description: Raw affinity rules (merged with nodeTargeting-generated rules). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints map. labelSelector defaults to chart's selectorLabels. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/topology-spread-constraints/

maxSkew.description: Maximum allowed difference in pod count between topology domains

topologyKey.description: Node label key defining the topology domain (e.g. topology.kubernetes.io/zone)

whenUnsatisfiable.description: DoNotSchedule prevents scheduling; ScheduleAnyway schedules anyway with skew minimization

minDomains.description: Minimum number of eligible topology domains (K8s 1.30+)

nodeAffinityPolicy.description: Whether to honor nodeAffinity when calculating skew

nodeTaintsPolicy.description: Whether to honor node taints when calculating skew

matchLabelKeys.description: Pod label keys to use for spreading (K8s 1.27+)

labelSelector.description: Override the pod label selector (defaults to chart's selectorLabels)

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClass name for pod scheduling priority. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Seconds to wait for graceful shutdown before force-killing (default: 30)

dnsConfig.description: Custom DNS settings (nameservers, searches, options). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dns-pod-service/#pod-dns-config

hostNetwork.description: Use the host's network namespace. The pod will use the node's IP and ports directly.

restartPolicy.description: Pod restart policy. Always for Deployments/StatefulSets, OnFailure or Never for Jobs. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/#restart-policy

services.description: Map of service name to service config

x-wizard.label: Services
x-wizard.description: Configure Kubernetes Services to expose your application.

type.description: Service type determining how the Service is exposed. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

headless.description: Create a headless Service (clusterIP: None), used for StatefulSets or direct pod DNS. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#headless-services

ports.description: Map of port name to port config

port.description: The port number exposed by the Service

containerPort.description: Container port name, defaults to port key name

nodePort.description: Static port on every node (only for NodePort/LoadBalancer type services, range 30000-32767)

loadBalancerIP.description: Request a specific IP for LoadBalancer type services (cloud-provider dependent)

loadBalancerSourceRanges.description: CIDR ranges allowed to access a LoadBalancer (e.g. ['10.0.0.0/8']). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#load-balancer-source-ranges

externalTrafficPolicy.description: Controls whether external traffic is routed to node-local or cluster-wide endpoints. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#external-traffic-policy

ipFamilies.description: IP families for the Service. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dual-stack/#services

publishNotReadyAddresses.description: If true, endpoints are published even when pods are not ready (useful for StatefulSet peer discovery)

selectorLabels.description: Override the default pod selector labels for this Service (defaults to chart's selectorLabels)

annotations.description: Annotations added to the Service resource. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/

labels.description: Extra labels added to the Service resource

oauth2Proxies.description: Map of oauth2 proxy name to proxy config

x-wizard.label: OAuth2 Proxy
x-wizard.description: Configure OAuth2 proxy for authentication (sidecar or separate deployment).

mode.description: sidecar runs in app pod; deployment creates separate pods. See https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/

issuerUrl.description: OIDC issuer URL for token validation (e.g. https://accounts.google.com). See https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/configuration/providers/openid_connect

emailDomains.description: Allowed email domains (* = allow all). See https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/configuration/overview

upstream.description: Upstream URL to proxy to (required for deployment mode, e.g. http://my-app-svc:8080). See https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/configuration/overview#upstreams-configuration

replicas.description: Number of proxy replicas (deployment mode only)

port.description: Port the OAuth2 proxy listens on

env.description: Environment variables (CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, COOKIE_SECRET are auto-injected as args). See https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/configuration/overview

extraArgs.description: Extra arguments as key-value pairs (e.g. cookie-refresh: '1h'). See https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/configuration/overview

args.description: Override the entire command arguments array

resources.description: CPU/memory resource requests and limits. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

annotations.description: Extra annotations on OAuth2 proxy resources

labels.description: Extra labels on OAuth2 proxy resources

ingresses.description: Map of ingress name to ingress config

x-wizard.label: Ingresses
x-wizard.description: Configure Kubernetes Ingresses for external access.

oauth2Proxy.description: Key from networking.oauth2Proxies map if oauth2proxy should be used for authentication on this Ingress

pathType.description: How the path is matched. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/ingress/#path-types

serviceName.description: Backend Service name (defaults to the first Service in networking.services)

items.description: Path rule mapping a URL path to a backend service

paths.description: URL path rules for this host

items.description: Host rule with hostname and path mappings

hosts.description: List of host rules for the Ingress

secretName.description: Name of the Secret containing tls.crt and tls.key

hosts.description: Hostnames covered by this TLS certificate

items.description: TLS entry mapping hosts to a Secret containing the certificate

annotations.description: Annotations on the Ingress (often used for controller-specific config)

labels.description: Extra labels on the Ingress resource

defaultBackend.description: Default backend for unmatched requests. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/ingress/#default-backend

x-wizard.label: Gateway API
x-wizard.description: Configure Gateway API routes (HTTPRoute, GRPCRoute, TLSRoute, etc.).

oauth2Proxy.description: Key from networking.oauth2Proxies map if oauth2proxy should be used for authentication on this route

sectionName.description: Specific listener name on the Gateway

port.description: Specific listener port on the Gateway

parentRefs.description: Gateway references this route attaches to. See https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/api-types/httproute/#attaching-to-gateways

circuitBreaker.description: Circuit breaker settings for upstream connections

healthCheck.description: Active health check configuration for backends

useClientProtocol.description: Use the same protocol as the downstream client

proxyProtocol.description: PROXY protocol configuration for backend connections

policies.description: Traffic policies attached to this route

networkPolicies.description: Map of NetworkPolicy name to config

x-wizard.label: Network Policies
x-wizard.description: Configure Kubernetes NetworkPolicies to control pod-to-pod traffic.

podSelector.description: Select which pods this policy applies to (defaults to chart's selectorLabels)

x-wizard.label: Istio
x-wizard.description: Configure Istio service mesh resources: VirtualServices, DestinationRules, PeerAuthentication, AuthorizationPolicies.

virtualServices.description: Map of VirtualService name to config

gateways.description: Gateways this VirtualService is bound to (e.g. ['istio-system/my-gateway'])

exportTo.description: Namespaces this VirtualService is visible to ('.' = same ns, '*' = all)

http.description: HTTP route rules (match, route, redirect, rewrite, etc.)

destinationRules.description: Map of DestinationRule name to config

host.description: Service key (auto-prefixed with release name) or FQDN if externalHost=true

externalHost.description: If true, the host value is used as-is (not prefixed with release name)

exportTo.description: Namespaces this rule is visible to

peerAuthentication.description: Map of PeerAuthentication name to config

authorizationPolicies.description: Map of AuthorizationPolicy name to config

action.description: Policy action: ALLOW, DENY, CUSTOM, or AUDIT

networking.description: Networking configuration: Services, Ingresses, Gateway API, mTLS, and network policies

x-wizard.label: ConfigMaps
x-wizard.description: Manage ConfigMaps. Chart-managed ConfigMaps are auto-prefixed and include checksum annotations for automatic pod rollouts.

data.description: Key-value pairs stored in the ConfigMap

binaryData.description: Binary data stored in the ConfigMap (base64-encoded values)

reloaderEnabled.description: Include this ConfigMap in Stakater Reloader annotations for auto pod restarts on changes. See https://github.com/stakater/Reloader

x-wizard.label: Secrets
x-wizard.description: Manage Secrets with optional auto-generation via ESO Password generators.

generate.description: Auto-generate random values via External Secrets Operator Password generator. See https://external-secrets.io/latest/provider/generator-password/

symbolCharacters.description: Allowed symbol characters (defaults to provider default)

encoding.description: Output encoding for the generated value

additionalProperties.description: Password generation config for a single key

generateRefreshInterval.description: How often ESO regenerates the secret. '0' = generate once and never rotate.

reloaderEnabled.description: Include in Stakater Reloader annotations for auto pod restarts. See https://github.com/stakater/Reloader

x-wizard.label: External Secrets
x-wizard.description: Configure ExternalSecrets to sync secrets from external stores (AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, etc.).

refreshInterval.description: How often to sync the secret from the external store (e.g. '1h', '5m'). See https://external-secrets.io/latest/api/externalsecret/

secretStoreRef.description: Reference to the SecretStore or ClusterSecretStore. See https://external-secrets.io/latest/api/secretstore/

creationPolicy.description: How the Secret is created. See https://external-secrets.io/latest/api/externalsecret/#target

deletionPolicy.description: What happens when the ExternalSecret is deleted. See https://external-secrets.io/latest/api/externalsecret/#target

template.description: Template for the generated Secret (type, metadata, data). See https://external-secrets.io/latest/guides/templating/

data.description: Individual key mappings from the external store. See https://external-secrets.io/latest/api/externalsecret/#data

secretKey.description: Key name in the target Kubernetes Secret

remoteRef.description: Reference to the key in the external store

key.description: Key/path in the external store (e.g. my-secret, /path/to/secret)

property.description: Property within the secret (for JSON secrets)

version.description: Version or stage of the secret

dataFrom.description: Bulk data extraction from the external store. See https://external-secrets.io/latest/api/externalsecret/#datafrom

certificates.description: Map of cert-manager Certificate name to config

x-wizard.label: Certificates
x-wizard.description: Configure cert-manager Certificate resources for TLS. Certificates auto-mount when a mount path is set.

issuerRef.description: Reference to the cert-manager Issuer or ClusterIssuer. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/configuration/

secretName.description: Target Secret name for the TLS cert (defaults to <fullname>-<key>-tls)

mountPath.description: Auto-mount the certificate Secret to all containers at this path

commonName.description: X.509 common name (CN) for the certificate

isCA.description: Mark this certificate as a Certificate Authority

duration.description: Certificate validity duration (e.g. 2160h for 90 days). See https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/certificate/#renewal

renewBefore.description: Time before expiry to begin renewal (e.g. 360h for 15 days)

dnsNames.description: Subject Alternative Names (SANs) — DNS entries the cert is valid for

ipAddresses.description: Subject Alternative Names (SANs) — IP addresses the cert is valid for

uris.description: Subject Alternative Names (SANs) — URIs the cert is valid for

privateKey.description: Private key configuration (algorithm, encoding, size, rotation). See https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/certificate/#private-key

secretTemplate.description: Template for the generated Secret (annotations, labels)

x-wizard.label: Persistence
x-wizard.description: Configure persistent storage. For StatefulSets, entries become volumeClaimTemplates.

storageClass.description: StorageClass name (e.g. gp3, standard). Empty = cluster default. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/storage-classes/

existingClaim.description: Use an existing PVC instead of creating a new one

volumeName.description: Bind to a specific PersistentVolume by name

selector.description: Label selector to filter available PersistentVolumes

additionalProperties.description: PersistentVolumeClaim configuration. For StatefulSets, these become volumeClaimTemplates. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/

x-wizard.label: Autoscaling
x-wizard.description: Configure HPA, VPA, and KEDA autoscaling. Note: HPA and KEDA are mutually exclusive.

behavior.description: Scale-up and scale-down behavior policies. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/#configurable-scaling-behavior

enabled.description: Enable VPA for this workload. See https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/tree/master/vertical-pod-autoscaler

updateMode.description: Auto: applies on pod restart; Recreate: evicts pods; Initial: only at creation; Off: recommendations only

mode.description: Auto: VPA manages this container; Off: VPA ignores it

enabled.description: Enable KEDA scaling for this workload

minReplicaCount.description: Minimum number of replicas (0 = scale to zero)

pollingInterval.description: How often KEDA checks triggers (seconds, default: 30)

cooldownPeriod.description: Wait period after last trigger before scaling down (seconds, default: 300)

idleReplicaCount.description: Replica count when idle (below minReplicaCount). See https://keda.sh/docs/latest/concepts/scaling-deployments/#idlereplicacount

fallback.description: Fallback replicas when trigger metrics are unavailable. See https://keda.sh/docs/latest/concepts/scaling-deployments/#fallback

type.description: Trigger type (e.g. prometheus, kafka, cron). See https://keda.sh/docs/latest/scalers/

metricType.description: Metric type: AverageValue, Value, or Utilization

items.description: A KEDA trigger (e.g. prometheus, kafka, rabbitmq, cron)

successfulJobsHistoryLimit.description: ScaledJob: how many successful jobs to keep (default: 100)

failedJobsHistoryLimit.description: ScaledJob: how many failed jobs to keep (default: 100)

scalingStrategy.description: ScaledJob: scaling strategy (default, custom, accurate). See https://keda.sh/docs/latest/concepts/scaling-jobs/

annotations.description: Annotations on the KEDA ScaledObject/ScaledJob resource

labels.description: Labels on the KEDA ScaledObject/ScaledJob resource

x-wizard.label: Pod Disruption Budget
x-wizard.description: Configure Pod Disruption Budget to ensure availability during voluntary disruptions.

enabled.description: Enable Pod Disruption Budget for this workload

minAvailable.description: Minimum pods that must be available during disruptions (number or percentage like '50%'). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/configure-pdb/

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum pods that can be unavailable during disruptions (number or percentage)

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: Policy for evicting unhealthy pods (K8s 1.31+). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/configure-pdb/#unhealthy-pod-eviction-policy

x-wizard.label: RBAC
x-wizard.description: Configure ServiceAccount, Roles, ClusterRoles, and their Bindings.

create.description: Create a ServiceAccount for this release

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Auto-mount the service account token in pods. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-service-account/#opt-out-of-api-credential-automounting

name.description: ServiceAccount name (defaults to fullname if create=true, or 'default')

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets to add to the ServiceAccount

annotations.description: Annotations on the ServiceAccount (e.g. for IAM role binding)

verbs.description: Allowed verbs (e.g. get, list, watch, create, update, delete)

roles.description: Map of Role name to config. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#role-and-clusterrole

verbs.description: Allowed verbs (e.g. get, list, watch, create, update, delete)

clusterRoles.description: Map of ClusterRole name to config. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#role-and-clusterrole

roleRef.description: Reference to the Role to bind. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#rolebinding-and-clusterrolebinding

subjects.description: Subjects to bind (defaults to chart's ServiceAccount)

roleBindings.description: Map of RoleBinding name to config. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#rolebinding-and-clusterrolebinding

roleRef.description: Reference to the ClusterRole to bind. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#rolebinding-and-clusterrolebinding

subjects.description: Subjects to bind (defaults to chart's ServiceAccount)

clusterRoleBindings.description: Map of ClusterRoleBinding name to config. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/#rolebinding-and-clusterrolebinding

monitors.description: Map of monitor name to monitor config

x-wizard.label: Monitors
x-wizard.description: Configure ServiceMonitor/PodMonitor for Prometheus or VictoriaMetrics.

kind.description: Monitor type: 'service' creates ServiceMonitor, 'pod' creates PodMonitor

operator.description: Target operator: 'prometheus' creates ServiceMonitor/PodMonitor, 'victoriametrics' creates VMServiceScrape/VMPodScrape

port.description: Named port to scrape (must match a port name in networking.services)

scrapeTimeout.description: Per-scrape timeout (must be less than interval)

honorLabels.description: Keep metric labels from the target even if they conflict with attached labels

honorTimestamps.description: Use timestamps from scraped metrics instead of scrape time

params.description: Optional HTTP query parameters for the scrape request

tlsConfig.description: TLS configuration for scraping HTTPS endpoints

bearerTokenSecret.description: Secret reference for bearer token authentication

proxyURL.description: HTTP proxy URL for scrape requests

metricRelabelings.description: Relabeling rules applied to scraped metrics before ingestion

relabelings.description: Relabeling rules applied to discovered targets before scraping

endpoints.description: Multiple endpoint configurations (overrides single-endpoint fields above)

selector.description: Override label selector for discovering target services/pods

namespaceSelector.description: Namespace selector for discovering targets across namespaces

jobLabel.description: Label whose value becomes the Prometheus job name (ServiceMonitor only)

targetLabels.description: Service labels to transfer to scraped metrics (ServiceMonitor only)

podTargetLabels.description: Pod labels to transfer to scraped metrics (PodMonitor only)

sampleLimit.description: Per-scrape limit on number of samples accepted

maxScrapeSize.description: Maximum size of a single scrape response (VictoriaMetrics only)

seriesLimit.description: Limit on unique time series per scrape target (VictoriaMetrics only)

additionalProperties.description: ServiceMonitor or PodMonitor configuration for Prometheus/VictoriaMetrics. See https://prometheus-operator.dev/docs/api-reference/api/#monitoring.coreos.com/v1.ServiceMonitor

alerting.description: Map of alerting rule name to config (PrometheusRule or VMRule)

x-wizard.label: Alerting Rules
x-wizard.description: Configure PrometheusRule or VMRule alerting and recording rules.

operator.description: Target operator: 'prometheus' creates PrometheusRule, 'victoriametrics' creates VMRule

groups.description: Rule groups in standard Prometheus format. See https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/recording_rules/

interval.description: Evaluation interval for rules in this group

limit.description: Maximum number of alerts produced by this group

labels.description: Labels added to all rules in this group

items.description: A rule group containing alerting or recording rules

x-wizard.label: Dashboards
x-wizard.description: Configure Grafana dashboards via the Grafana Operator. See https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/as-code/infrastructure-as-code/grafana-operator/operator-dashboards-folders-datasources/#add-a-dashboard-to-a-folder

grafana.description: Map of Grafana dashboard name to dashboard config

instanceSelector.description: Label selector to match target Grafana instances. See https://grafana.github.io/grafana-operator/docs/dashboards/#instance-selector

matchLabels.description: Labels that must match the Grafana instance (e.g. dashboards: enabled)

grafanaCom.description: Import from grafana.com by dashboard ID. See https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/

id.description: Dashboard ID from grafana.com (e.g. 1860 for Node Exporter)

url.description: URL to fetch dashboard JSON from. See https://grafana.github.io/grafana-operator/docs/dashboards/#from-url

configMapRef.description: Load dashboard from a ConfigMap. See https://grafana.github.io/grafana-operator/docs/dashboards/#from-configmap

key.description: Key within the ConfigMap containing the dashboard JSON

folderUID.description: Grafana folder UID (takes precedence over folder name)

folderRef.description: Reference to a GrafanaFolder resource name

uid.description: Explicit dashboard UID (max 40 chars, auto-generated if omitted)

resyncPeriod.description: How often to re-sync the dashboard (e.g. '5m')

allowCrossNamespaceImport.description: Allow import by Grafana instances in other namespaces

urlAuthorization.description: Authorization config for fetching dashboard from URL

jsonnet.description: Jsonnet source for generating the dashboard. See https://grafana.github.io/grafana-operator/docs/dashboards/#from-jsonnet

contentCacheDuration.description: How long to cache fetched dashboard content

inputName.description: Variable name in the dashboard JSON (e.g. DS_PROMETHEUS)

envs.description: Environment variable substitutions in the dashboard JSON

additionalProperties.description: GrafanaDashboard configuration via Grafana Operator. See https://grafana.github.io/grafana-operator/docs/dashboards/

x-wizard.label: Workload Settings
x-wizard.description: Configure settings specific to your workload type (replicas, strategy, schedule, etc.).

strategy.description: Rollout strategy: canary or blueGreen. See https://argo-rollouts.readthedocs.io/en/stable/features/canary/

minReadySeconds.description: Minimum seconds a pod must be ready before considered available. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#min-ready-seconds

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Number of old ReplicaSets to retain for rollback. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#revision-history-limit

restartAt.description: Scheduled restart time in RFC 3339 format

type.description: RollingUpdate replaces pods gradually; Recreate kills all existing pods first. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#strategy

minReadySeconds.description: Minimum seconds a pod must be ready before considered available. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#min-ready-seconds

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Number of old ReplicaSets to retain for rollback. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#revision-history-limit

serviceName.description: Override the governing headless Service name

publishNotReadyAddresses.description: Publish DNS records for pods even when not ready

podManagementPolicy.description: OrderedReady creates pods sequentially; Parallel launches all at once. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/statefulset/#pod-management-policies

type.description: RollingUpdate updates pods automatically; OnDelete waits for manual pod deletion. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/statefulset/#update-strategies

minReadySeconds.description: Minimum seconds a pod must be ready before considered available. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#min-ready-seconds

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Number of old revisions to retain for rollback. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#revision-history-limit

persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy.description: PVC retention policy on scale-down/deletion. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/statefulset/#persistentvolumeclaim-retention

type.description: RollingUpdate updates pods automatically; OnDelete waits for manual pod deletion. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/manage-daemon/update-daemon-set/

minReadySeconds.description: Minimum seconds a pod must be ready before considered available. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#min-ready-seconds

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Number of old DaemonSet revisions to retain for rollback. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#revision-history-limit

schedule.description: Cron schedule expression (e.g. '0 */6 * * *'). Use https://crontab.guru to build expressions.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone for the schedule (e.g. 'America/New_York'). See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

concurrencyPolicy.description: Allow runs concurrent jobs; Forbid skips if previous is running; Replace kills previous job first

suspend.description: Suspend subsequent executions (does not apply to already started jobs)

successfulJobsHistoryLimit.description: Number of successful finished jobs to retain (default: 3)

failedJobsHistoryLimit.description: Number of failed finished jobs to retain (default: 1)

startingDeadlineSeconds.description: Deadline in seconds for starting the job if it missed its schedule

backoffLimit.description: Number of retries before marking the job as failed (default: 6)

activeDeadlineSeconds.description: Maximum duration in seconds before the job is terminated

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Seconds to keep the job after it finishes. Cleaned up by TTL controller. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/ttlafterfinished/

parallelism.description: Maximum number of pods running in parallel (default: 1)

completionMode.description: NonIndexed: any pod completion counts. Indexed: each index must complete. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#completion-mode

podReplacementPolicy.description: When to create replacement pods (K8s 1.34+). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#pod-replacement-policy

suspend.description: Suspend the job — running pods will be terminated and restarted when resumed

hooks.description: Map of hook name to hook Job config. Runs at Helm lifecycle events.

x-wizard.label: Hooks
x-wizard.description: Configure Helm lifecycle hooks (pre-install, pre-upgrade, etc.) that run as Jobs.

events.description: Helm lifecycle events that trigger this hook. See https://helm.sh/docs/topics/charts_hooks/#the-available-hooks

weight.description: Execution order among hooks at the same event (lower runs first). See https://helm.sh/docs/topics/charts_hooks/#hook-weights

deletePolicy.description: When to delete the hook resource. See https://helm.sh/docs/topics/charts_hooks/#hook-deletion-policies

containers.description: Hook Job containers (same format as main containers). See https://helm.sh/docs/topics/charts_hooks/

initContainers.description: Init containers for the hook Job

backoffLimit.description: Number of retries before marking the hook Job as failed. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#pod-backoff-failure-policy

activeDeadlineSeconds.description: Maximum duration (seconds) before the hook Job is terminated. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#job-termination-and-cleanup

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Seconds to keep the hook Job after completion. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#ttl-mechanism-for-finished-jobs

completions.description: Number of successful completions required. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#parallel-jobs

parallelism.description: Maximum number of pods running in parallel. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/#parallel-jobs

annotations.description: Extra annotations on the hook Job

labels.description: Extra labels on the hook Job

additionalProperties.description: Helm hook Job configuration. See https://helm.sh/docs/topics/charts_hooks/

x-wizard.label: Extra Resources
x-wizard.description: Add arbitrary Kubernetes resources not covered by the chart.

extraResources.description: Arbitrary Kubernetes resources not covered by the chart. Each item is a complete resource manifest.
